question: Modify the `Book` class (from Q1) to include a default constructor (initializes with empty strings), a parameterized constructor, and a copy constructor. Demonstrate creating objects using all three types of constructors.

test_cases.expected:
Book 1 (Default): Title: , Author: 
Book 2 (Parameterized): Title: 1984, Author: George Orwell
Book 3 (Copy of Book 2): Title: 1984, Author: George Orwell
Book 4 (Copy of Book 1): Title: , Author:

errors: Constructors initialize `std::string` members in the constructor body using assignment instead of member initializer lists. This is less efficient because the `std::string` members are first default-constructed, then assigned, leading to redundant operations.
Unnecessary use of `this->` to access member variables within constructors and the `display` method, which adds verbosity without improving clarity or functionality.
